subject: hamsters
one_liner: Split any purchase into four payments your hamster can technically afford.
brand_voice_notes: Deadpan consumer-credit fintech that has, with total sincerity, decided rodents are a borrower segment. Friendly and reassuring on the surface, mildly predatory underneath. Treats a two-year lifespan as a routine underwriting variable. Never winks. Talks about cheek-pouch capacity and wheel mileage the way a real lender talks about income and credit history.

hero.sub: Hoard splits any purchase into four payments and quietly amortizes the rest across your hamster's remaining lifespan. No interest. No paperwork your hamster can read.

features.title: Cheek-pouch underwriting
features.blurb: We set credit limits by measuring how much your hamster can physically carry at once. Two full pouches, two thousand dollars. The model is non-negotiable and faintly impressed.

features.title: Your live HoardScore
features.blurb: A creditworthiness score from 0 to 850 that rewards wheel mileage and penalizes naps. Most hamsters sleep through their best rate and we are not able to wake them on their behalf.

features.title: Lifespan amortization
features.blurb: Every purchase spreads across your hamster's projected remaining months, recalculated each night you confirm the borrower is still with us. Miss a confirmation and the balance accelerates.

pricing_tiers.name: Pellet
perks: An email the moment a payment is missed

pricing_tiers.name: Burrow
perks: Dispute resolution mediated between you and the hamster

pricing_tiers.name: Whole Colony
pricing_tiers.tagline: For operators with a cage system.
perks: Underwrite up to 24 hamsters at once
A dedicated account manager who has met a hamster

testimonials.quote: I financed a second wheel before I had paid off the first. At no point was I stopped.
testimonials.name: Biscuit
testimonials.title: Syrian Hamster, Series A Borrower

testimonials.quote: My HoardScore fell forty points during a long nap. I am formally disputing the methodology.
testimonials.name: Sir Reginald Pellingsworth III
testimonials.title: Roborovski Dwarf, Two-Time Defaulter

testimonials.quote: They approved my habitat expansion in under four seconds. I have not read the terms and I will not be reading them.
testimonials.name: Noodle
testimonials.title: Winter White, Joint Account Holder

demo_screen_concept: A buy-now-pay-later checkout for a hamster's shopping cart. A list of cart items each with a price (Deluxe Silent Wheel, 5kg sunflower-seed sack, modular tube expansion, ceramic hideout, cedar bedding bale). A live 'Split into 4' panel showing four dated installments that recompute the instant you toggle an item in or out of the cart. A circular HoardScore gauge from 0 to 850 that ticks up or down as the projected balance changes. An 'Approve installment plan' button that opens a confirmation modal asking the operator to verify the hamster is still alive before funds release.

images.rationale: Typographic claymorphic hero is CSS/SVG-driven, so a photographic hero was skipped (a real photo would fight the soft-toy clay surfaces). The demo is a BNPL checkout / payment-schedule screen, which is document-shaped not photo-shaped, so no demo subject assets. Full budget went to the always-applicable fallback: three deadpan corporate headshots of the subject (hamsters) for the named testimonials.

items.prompt: Studio corporate headshot photograph of a single golden Syrian hamster, calm businesslike expression, head-and-shoulders, warm toasted-oat #F4EDE0 backdrop, soft coral #E25C3B rim light, realistic photography, no text/letters/numbers/logos/watermarks/UI/clothing/props.

Studio corporate headshot photograph of a single Roborovski dwarf hamster with pale white eyebrow markings, stern skeptical expression, head-and-shoulders, warm toasted-oat #F4EDE0 backdrop, realistic photography, no text/letters/numbers/logos/watermarks/UI/clothing/props.

Studio corporate headshot photograph of a single Winter White dwarf hamster, plush grey-and-white fur, placid expression, head-and-shoulders, warm toasted-oat #F4EDE0 backdrop, soft coral #E25C3B rim light, realistic photography, no text/letters/numbers/logos/watermarks/UI/clothing/props.

design_direction.style: Claymorphic consumer-fintech, light warm-mode. A soft toasted-oat ground (the color of clean bedding) carries inflated, rounded clay surfaces: every card, button, badge and input is a puffy extruded shape with a dual soft-shadow treatment (a light highlight from the top-left, a soft brown ambient shadow to the bottom-right) and generous 22-32px radii. One clay teal-green carries brand, structure, links and the HoardScore meter; one warm coral is rationed strictly for the single primary CTA, the live 'approve' affordance, and the late-payment signal, and appears almost nowhere else. An oversized rounded Fredoka headline does the heavy lifting; Nunito carries warm, friendly body copy. Every figure that pretends to be money (prices, the four installment amounts, HoardScore, projected balances) is set in Nunito with tabular numerals so columns stay honest, deliberately warm rather than a cold console monospace. Buttons physically depress on press, shadows softening as they sink. No gradients beyond a faint top vignette, no glassmorphism, no neon, no flat hard-edged cards, no monospace telemetry. Reads like a friendly, well-funded consumer-lending app that has been built, with total sincerity, to extend credit to an animal that lives roughly two years.
design_direction.archetype: Consumer-fintech / BNPL landing: hero with floating clay payment-schedule card, how-it-works features, social proof, three pricing tiers, footer.
design_direction.motion_vocab: Soft spring presses (scale 0.97 with shadow softening on :active), gentle 180ms ease-out hovers that lift clay surfaces a few pixels, a HoardScore needle that eases to its value. No parallax, no scroll-jacking; all motion respects prefers-reduced-motion.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Oversized Fredoka 'Buy now. Hoard later.' headline flush-left, warm Nunito subhead, one coral CTA pill. To the right, a puffy clay card floats showing a sample purchase split into four dated payments with a small HoardScore chip, sitting on dual soft shadows. Soft toasted-oat background, a few faint clay 'seed' blobs for texture.
